purpose: Gate the HipKittens route-keyed D512 row-term ART dQ path after changing the output from slice scratch to production-shaped [B,H,S,D] dQ over all q_tile x kv_tile pairs.
timed_boundary: D512 score/row-term producer plus full-sequence ART dQ output gate: QK and dO V^T are accumulated over four D128 chunks before row terms for every q_tile x kv_tile pair; the dP page feeds ART K^T @ dP consumers and atomically accumulates BF16 transposed 16x32 dQ tiles into [B,H,S,D]. No dOriKV, dCmpKV, or production dSinks are emitted yet.
decision: Rejected as a full-boundary direction. The H=1 path proves the ART dQ output contract is finite, but the H=64 C4 scale gate is 70.48 ms before dOriKV/dCmpKV/dSinks are even attached, far above the current Triton C4 SharedKV boundary. The issue is schedule/layout, not validity: this gate launches per-head q_tile x kv_tile work with full dQ atomics and synthetic per-head K/V tensors, while the target needs a true shared-KV H64/N64-ish source-boundary owner that keeps the fast Triton head-block/query-major schedule and only adds route-aware dCmpKV epilogue.
next_step: Do not extend this per-head fullseq owner. Rebase the HipKittens/HIP rewrite around the Triton schedule spec: one source-boundary producer for H64/N64/D128-style blocks, shared K/V source layout, dQ plus dOriKV output, dSinks folded into row terms, and route-aware compressed dCmpKV reduction before global accumulation.
